reasoning: Five MEPs (Martusciello, Princi, De Meo - EPP Italy; Attard - S&D Malta; Minchev - Renew Bulgaria) face immunity lift requests and criminal investigation by Belgian prosecutors in Huawei bribery probe. Princi's immunity request was withdrawn shortly after being made due to exonerating evidence. The investigation is serious (immunity lift requests, arrests of assistants), resembling Qatargate which led to resignations. With five individuals implicated, even a modest 15% individual probability yields ~55% chance at least one resigns. However, the June 9-30 window is very narrow (22 days), and typical MEP behavior is to fight charges rather than resign. Princi's withdrawal suggests some may be exonerated. Still, with 5 involved and serious criminal charges pending, plus precedent from Qatargate, the probability is elevated to 55%.
